mechanism_narrative: Higher trailing 3-month growth in nominal retail sales reflects stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum, producing a positive relation with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 689443 as of the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward coefficient on 3-month RSAFS growth is negative or if triggered high-growth retail-sales observations do not outperform low-growth observations over 20 trading days in more than 55% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the federal funds rate increases discount rates and tightens financial conditions, lowering equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 12-month moving average by more than 100 bp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 3.78 as of the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations after the FEDFUNDS crossing condition, or if the post-trigger mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth weakens while unemployment rises, labor income expectations deteriorate and recession risk increases, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage point over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 3.7 as of the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between rising UNRATE and decelerating PAYEMS has a nonnegative estimated effect, or if 60-day returns after the trigger exceed the unconditional median in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals weakening goods-sector output and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses below 0 after being positive in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 104.5175 as of the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns following negative INDPRO 3-month growth are not lower than returns following positive INDPRO 3-month growth, or if the sign of the estimated effect is positive in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts during a high-rate environment indicate weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction income, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year growth crosses below -10% while FEDFUNDS is above 3%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 1425 as of two months prior
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling HOUST and high FEDFUNDS is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances or if HOUST weakness has no incremental effect conditional on FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising policy rates forces investors to price a more restrictive central-bank reaction function, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is above 5% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 298.349 as of the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction has a positive return coefficient, or if high-inflation rising-rate observations do not underperform other observations over 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment with continuing payroll gains signals resilient household income and near-term consumption support, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 4% and PAYEMS 1-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value was 153548 as of the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns following low-UNRATE positive-PAYEMS observations are below the unconditional 5-day return median in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real GDP reflects broad demand growth and improving aggregate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses above 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value was 20039.406 as of five months prior
failure_condition: Reject if observations after GDPC1 growth crosses above 2% have average 60-day SPX returns below the unconditional average, or if the positive GDP-growth coefficient is unstable across more than half of rolling 5-year windows.
